G4102.semantic_range: persuasion, i.e. credence; moral conviction (of religious truth, or the truthfulness of God or a religious teacher), especially reliance upon Christ for salvation; abstractly, constancy in such profession; by extension, the system of religious (Gospel) truth itself

dodson.def: persuasion, i.e. credence; moral conviction (of religious truth, or the truthfulness of God or a religious teacher), especially reliance upon Christ for salvation; abstractly, constancy in such profession; by extension, the system of religious (Gospel) truth itself

thayer.short: from 3982; persuasion, i.e. credence; moral conviction (of religious truth, or the truthfulness of God or a religious teacher), especially reliance upon Christ for salvation; abstractly, constancy in…
thayer.long: from 3982; persuasion, i.e. credence; moral conviction (of religious truth, or the truthfulness of God or a religious teacher), especially reliance upon Christ for salvation; abstractly, constancy in such profession; by extension, the system of religious (Gospel) truth itself:--assurance, belief, believe, faith, fidelity. see GREEK for 3982

dodson.deriv: from a primary (to parcel out, especially food or grazing to animals);

thayer.short: from a primary nemo (to parcel out, especially food or grazing to animals); law (through the idea of prescriptive usage), genitive case (regulation), specially, (of Moses (including the volume); also…
thayer.long: from a primary nemo (to parcel out, especially food or grazing to animals); law (through the idea of prescriptive usage), genitive case (regulation), specially, (of Moses (including the volume); also of the Gospel), or figuratively (a principle):--law.

thayer.long: from 25; love, i.e. affection or benevolence; specially (plural) a love-feast:--(feast of) charity(-ably), dear, love. see GREEK for 25

G4561.semantic_range: flesh (as stripped of the skin), i.e. (strictly) the meat of an animal (as food), or (by extension) the body (as opposed to the soul (or spirit), or as the symbol of what is external, or as the means of kindred), or (by implication) human nature (with its frailties (physically or morally) and passions), or (specially), a human being (as such)

dodson.def: flesh (as stripped of the skin), i.e. (strictly) the meat of an animal (as food), or (by extension) the body (as opposed to the soul (or spirit), or as the symbol of what is external, or as the means of kindred), or (by implication) human nature (with its frailties (physically or morally) and passio
dodson.deriv: probably from the base of G4563 (σαρόω);

thayer.short: probably from the base of 4563; flesh (as stripped of the skin), i.e. (strictly) the meat of an animal (as food), or (by extension) the body (as opposed to the soul (or spirit), or as the symbol of…
thayer.long: probably from the base of 4563; flesh (as stripped of the skin), i.e. (strictly) the meat of an animal (as food), or (by extension) the body (as opposed to the soul (or spirit), or as the symbol of what is external, or as the means of kindred), or (by implication) human nature (with its frailties (physically or morally) and passions), or (specially), a human being (as such):--carnal(-ly, + -ly min

G4151.semantic_range: a current of air, i.e. breath (blast) or a breeze; by analogy or figuratively, a spirit, i.e. (human) the rational soul, (by implication) vital principle, mental disposition, etc., or (superhuman) an angel, demon, or (divine) God, Christ's spirit, the Holy Spirit

dodson.def: a current of air, i.e. breath (blast) or a breeze; by analogy or figuratively, a spirit, i.e. (human) the rational soul, (by implication) vital principle, mental disposition, etc., or (superhuman) an angel, demon, or (divine) God, Christ's spirit, the Holy Spirit

thayer.short: from 4154; a current of air, i.e. breath (blast) or a breeze; by analogy or figuratively, a spirit, i.e. (human) the rational soul, (by implication) vital principle, mental disposition, etc., or…
thayer.long: from 4154; a current of air, i.e. breath (blast) or a breeze; by analogy or figuratively, a spirit, i.e. (human) the rational soul, (by implication) vital principle, mental disposition, etc., or (superhuman) an angel, demon, or (divine) God, Christ's spirit, the Holy Spirit:--ghost, life, spirit(-ual, -ually), mind. Compare 5590. see GREEK for 4154 see GREEK for 5590

G1342.semantic_range: equitable (in character or act); by implication, innocent, holy (absolutely or relatively)

dodson.def: equitable (in character or act); by implication, innocent, holy (absolutely or relatively)

thayer.short: from 1349; equitable (in character or act); by implication, innocent, holy (absolutely or relatively):--just, meet, right(-eous). see GREEK for
thayer.long: from 1349; equitable (in character or act); by implication, innocent, holy (absolutely or relatively):--just, meet, right(-eous). see GREEK for 1349

H430.semantic_range: gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used (in the plural thus, especially with the article) of the supreme God; occasionally applied by way of deference to magistrates; and sometimes as a superlative

hebrew.gloss: angels, [idiom] exceeding, God (gods) (-dess, -ly), [idiom] (very) great, judges, [idiom] mighty.
hebrew.def: gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used (in the plural thus, especially with the article) of the supreme God; occasionally applied by way of deference to magistrates; and sometimes as a superlative

bdb.long: gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used (in the plural thus, especially with the article) of the supreme God; occasionally applied by way of deference to magistrates; and sometimes as a superlative; angels, × exceeding, God (gods) (-dess, -ly), × (very) great, judges, × mighty.

lxx_bridge.note: Standard rendering; singular θεός for the grammatically plural אֱלֹהִים — LXX resolves the tension in favour of monotheism
In Ps 8:5 and divine council passages — "angels/heavenly beings"; used when אֱלֹהִים clearly refers to the heavenly court

H1285.semantic_range: a compact (because made by passing between pieces of flesh)

hebrew.def: a compact (because made by passing between pieces of flesh)
hebrew.deriv: from H1262 (בָּרָה) (in the sense of cutting (like H1254 (בָּרָא)));

bdb.long: a compact (because made by passing between pieces of flesh); confederacy, (con-) feder(-ate), covenant, league.

lxx_bridge.note: Near-exclusive rendering — but διαθήκη normally means "last will/testament" in secular Greek, not a bilateral covenant; this shaped the NT phrase "new covenant" toward a testamentary reading

H2617.semantic_range: kindness; by implication (towards God) piety; rarely (by opposition) reproof, or (subject.) beauty

hebrew.gloss: favour, good deed(-liness, -ness), kindly, (loving-) kindness, merciful (kindness), mercy, pity, reproach, wicked thing.
hebrew.def: kindness; by implication (towards God) piety; rarely (by opposition) reproof, or (subject.) beauty

bdb.long: kindness; by implication (towards God) piety; rarely (by opposition) reproof, or (subject.) beauty; favour, good deed(-liness, -ness), kindly, (loving-) kindness, merciful (kindness), mercy, pity, reproach, wicked thing.

lxx_bridge.note: Primary LXX rendering — captures mercy/compassion but loses the covenantal-loyalty dimension of חֶסֶד
Used where the relational grace/favour aspect is foregrounded over obligation
Rare — occurs where חֶסֶד appears in parallelism with צְדָקָה

H3068.semantic_range: Jehovah, Jewish national name of God

hebrew.gloss: Jehovah, the Lord. Compare H3050 (יָהּ), H3069 (יְהֹוִה).
hebrew.def: Jehovah, Jewish national name of God

bdb.long: (the) self-Existent or Eternal; Jeho-vah, Jewish national name of God; Jehovah, the Lord. Compare 3050, 3069.

lxx_bridge.note: "Lord" — substitution for the divine Name following Second Temple practice; obscures the personal-name character of יהוה and shaped NT Christology when applied to Jesus

H5315.semantic_range: properly, a breathing creature, i.e. animal of (abstractly) vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense (bodily or mental)

hebrew.gloss: any, appetite, beast, body, breath, creature, [idiom] dead(-ly), desire, [idiom] (dis-) contented, [idiom] fish, ghost, [phrase] greedy, he, heart(-y), (hath, [idiom] jeopardy of) life ([idiom] in jeopardy), lust, man, me, mind, mortally, one, own, person, pleasure, (her-, him-, my-, thy-) self, them (your) -selves, [phrase] slay, soul, [phrase] tablet, they, thing, ([idiom] she) will, [idiom] would have it.
hebrew.def: properly, a breathing creature, i.e. animal of (abstractly) vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense (bodily or mental)

bdb.long: properly, a breathing creature, i.e. animal of (abstractly) vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense (bodily or mental); any, appetite, beast, body, breath, creature, × dead(-ly), desire, × (dis-) contented, × fish, ghost,   greedy, he, heart(-y), (hath, × jeopardy of) life (× in jeopardy), lust, man, me, mind, mortally, one, own, person, pleasure, (her-, him-, my-

lxx_bridge.note: Dominant rendering — but ψυχή imports Greek dualism; נֶפֶשׁ refers to the whole living person, not an immortal soul housed in a body
Used when נֶפֶשׁ clearly means biological life

H5769.semantic_range: properly, concealed, i.e. the vanishing point; generally, time out of mind (past or future), i.e. (practically) eternity; frequentatively, adverbial (especially with prepositional prefix) always

hebrew.gloss: alway(-s), ancient (time), any more, continuance, eternal, (for, (n-)) ever(-lasting, -more, of old), lasting, long (time), (of) old (time), perpetual, at any time, (beginning of the) world ([phrase] without end). Compare H5331 (נֶצַח), H5703 (עַד).
hebrew.def: properly, concealed, i.e. the vanishing point; generally, time out of mind (past or future), i.e. (practically) eternity; frequentatively, adverbial (especially with prepositional prefix) always

bdb.long: properly, concealed, i.e. the vanishing point; generally, time out of mind (past or future), i.e. (practically) eternity; frequentatively, adverbial (especially with prepositional prefix) always; alway(-s), ancient (time), any more, continuance, eternal, (for, (n-)) ever(-lasting, -more, of old), lasting, long (time), (of) old (time), perpetual, at any time, (beginning of the) world (  without end

lxx_bridge.note: "Age" or "eternal duration" — the LXX equation of עוֹלָם with αἰών drove the NT phrase εἰς τοὺς αἰῶνας and the debate over "eternal" vs. "age-long"
Adjectival form — "ζωὴ αἰώνιος" (eternal life) translates the Hebrew concept of enduring life in the coming age

H6666.semantic_range: rightness (abstractly), subjectively (rectitude), objectively (justice), morally (virtue) or figuratively (prosperity)

hebrew.def: rightness (abstractly), subjectively (rectitude), objectively (justice), morally (virtue) or figuratively (prosperity)

bdb.long: rightness (abstractly), subjectively (rectitude), objectively (justice), morally (virtue) or figuratively (prosperity); justice, moderately, right(-eous) (act, -ly, -ness).

lxx_bridge.note: Primary rendering — legal/moral rightness; loses the sense of concrete saving acts that צְדָקָה often carries in Isaiah
Occurs when צְדָקָה appears alongside חֶסֶד — translators read both as aspects of covenant faithfulness
"Almsgiving/charity" — reflects post-exilic narrowing of צְדָקָה toward concrete acts of generosity

H7307.semantic_range: wind; by resemblance breath, i.e. a sensible (or even violent) exhalation; figuratively, life, anger, unsubstantiality; by extension, a region of the sky; by resemblance spirit, but only of a rational being (including its expression and functions)

hebrew.gloss: air, anger, blast, breath, [idiom] cool, courage, mind, [idiom] quarter, [idiom] side, spirit(-ual), tempest, [idiom] vain, (whirl-) wind(-y).
hebrew.def: wind; by resemblance breath, i.e. a sensible (or even violent) exhalation; figuratively, life, anger, unsubstantiality; by extension, a region of the sky; by resemblance spirit, but only of a rational being (including its expression and functions)

bdb.long: wind; by resemblance breath, i.e. a sensible (or even violent) exhalation; figuratively, life, anger, unsubstantiality; by extension, a region of the sky; by resemblance spirit, but only of a rational being (including its expression and functions); air, anger, blast, breath, × cool, courage, mind, × quarter, × side, spirit(-ual), tempest, × vain, (whirl-) wind(-y).

lxx_bridge.note: Used for both Spirit of God and human spirit — same lexical ambiguity as the Hebrew
Used when physical wind is the clear referent (weather contexts)
